highlights: Anthropic has withheld the release of its "Mythos" model because it demonstrates a professional human-level ability to discover and chain together complex security vulnerabilities in decades-old software.

The industry is shifting toward Small Language Models (SLMs) that use "distillation"—a process where larger models train smaller ones—to perform 90% of common tasks on local hardware at a fraction of the cost of frontier APIs.

AI is driving a period of "hyper-deflation" where the plummeting cost of compute and the rise of specialized, task-specific models may eventually make basic digital intelligence nearly free to access.

As core AI models become more capable, the primary moats for startups are shifting away from software "wrappers" toward hardware, network effects, and deep scientific IP that cannot be easily replicated by code.
summary:
The central focus of the transcript is Anthropic’s unveiling of "Mythos," a model so advanced in its ability to exploit and chain software vulnerabilities that the company has opted for a restricted release. Through "Project Glasswing," Anthropic is partnering with tech giants like Nvidia and AWS to use Mythos defensively to harden digital infrastructure before it can be weaponized by bad actors. The hosts and guests debate the geopolitical implications of this "cyber super-weapon," suggesting it marks a shift toward an AI arms race that may necessitate government involvement or a "Manhattan Project" style approach to national security.

The conversation shifts to the rise of Small Language Models (SLMs) and their role in a "hyper-deflationary" economy. Rob May of Neurometric explains that as "intelligence density" increases, smaller models (under 20 billion parameters) can handle up to 90% of common business tasks at a fraction of the cost of frontier models. This transition allows companies to drastically reduce expenses by using expensive models only for high-level reasoning while delegating routine work to specialized SLMs. The guests suggest this could lead to a collapse in the market value of frontier models as specialized "harness engineering" makes smaller, local models "good enough" for the majority of enterprise needs.

Finally, the episode explores startup defensibility through a tool called "Death by Claude," which roasts companies based on how easily they could be replaced by advancing AI. The consensus is that simple "AI wrappers" face terminal risk as the underlying models become more capable. To survive this era of rapid disruption, the guests identify hardware, network effects, and deep scientific IP as the primary remaining moats. The overarching theme is that while the cost of intelligence is plummeting toward zero, the value of resiliency, physical atoms, and human-centric services is becoming more critical for long-term business survival.
description:
Anthropic just built a model so dangerous, they’re not even going to release it (for now). On TWiST, Jason, Alex, and Neurometric CEO Rob May discuss whether or not this is just another frontier model or a potential weapon of mass internet destruction.

Claude Mythos Preview is already in the hands of over 40 Anthropic partners — including Nvidia, Apple, Amazon, Microsoft, and Google — as part of its “Project Glasswing” security council.

Together, they’ll test how quickly and easily the model can thwart today’s most elite cybersecurity protocols, and try to develop fresh protocols to keep our most sensitive and critical software systems safe and intact. Once Mythos or a similarly powerful model hits the streets… it’s all over!

Plus, we’re checking out the real-time SaaSpocalypse monitor Death By Clawd, and chatting with its creator, Gyani, about how it feels to have your startup one-shotted by Anthropic’s latest model.

"By 2030, 90% of common work tasks will be able to be done by a 10 billion parameter model or smaller." — Rob May

"Lovable built an AI that writes code for you, which is adorable — because Claude already writes code for you without the $20 per month middleman 
tax." — Death by Clawd
